word_list.word: a voice crying in the wilderness

senses.definition: someone who expresses an opinion that others ignore or do not like

examples.x: She is a voice crying in the wilderness, advocating for environmental sustainability in a world that often prioritizes short-term gains.
He remains a voice crying in the wilderness, speaking out against corruption and advocating for transparency in government.
Despite facing opposition, she was a voice crying in the wilderness, tirelessly fighting for gender equality decades before it gained mainstream attention.

senses.definition: used to refer to something that is done in a way that is contrary to the usual customs, conventions, or the natural direction

examples.x: They are currently swimming against the grain by starting their own business.
John has always been against the grain, expressing his unique viewpoints on various topics.
The professor's teaching methods are against the grain, focusing on student engagement rather than lectures.

senses.definition: someone who does not behave according to rules or expectations of society

examples.x: She acts like a law unto herself, always doing things her own way.
The artist is known for being a law unto himself, ignoring traditional artistic conventions.
They live their life as a law unto themselves, disregarding societal expectations.
He was a law unto himself, making decisions without considering others' opinions.
The CEO ran the company as a law unto herself, disregarding industry standards.
Despite criticism, she lived as a law unto herself, following her own path.
Tomorrow, he will continue to be a law unto himself, making choices based on his own values.
They are determined to live as a law unto themselves, pursuing their dreams against all odds.
In the future, she plans to assert herself as a law unto herself, standing up for what she believes in.

senses.definition: (of a person's words or actions) not according to what is considered acceptable or polite

examples.x: Her recent conduct is beyond the pale.
His opinions are entirely beyond the pale.
The language my father used was beyond the pale.

senses.definition: to act so differently that it changes traditional or previous methods or approaches

examples.x: She breaks the mold with her unique fashion sense and refuses to follow trends.
The company is known for breaking the mold in the tech industry by introducing revolutionary products.
They break the mold in the music industry with their experimental sound and genre-blending approach.
He broke the mold with his groundbreaking research that challenged long-held scientific theories.
Tomorrow, she will break the mold by launching a startup that disrupts the traditional business model.
In the future, he plans to break the mold in the fashion industry with his avant-garde designs.
They could break the mold if they dare to think differently and take risks in their entrepreneurial endeavors.

senses.definition: to show a behavior that is unacceptable or improper

examples.x: She consistently crosses the line by making personal attacks during arguments.
The comedian often crosses the line with their controversial jokes, causing outrage among certain audiences.
He crossed the line when he betrayed his friend's trust by sharing confidential information.
The politician crossed the line with their offensive remarks, resulting in widespread public backlash.
Tomorrow, they will cross a line if they proceed with their plan to discriminate against certain employees.
If she crosses a line again, there will be serious consequences for her actions.

senses.definition: beyond the limits of acceptable behavior

examples.x: He consistently goes out of bounds with his offensive and disrespectful comments.
The student is frequently out of bounds with their disruptive behavior in the classroom.
She went out of bounds when she cheated on the exam and got caught.
The company acted out of bounds by manipulating financial records to deceive investors.
If he continues his behavior, he will go out of bounds and face severe consequences.
If they go out of bounds again, serious disciplinary action will be taken.

senses.definition: to act in a way that is not in accordance with rules or socially accepted behavior

examples.x: He consistently steps out of line by disregarding company policies and protocols.
The student often steps out of line by interrupting the teacher and causing disruptions in the classroom.
They frequently step out of line with their disrespectful comments and behavior towards their colleagues.
She stepped out of line when she publicly criticized her supervisor during the staff meeting.
The employee stepped out of line by leaking confidential information to a competitor.
His comments during the meeting were out of line and offended many attendees.
The customer's rude and abusive behavior towards the staff was out of line and not tolerated.

senses.definition: something such as tradition, idea, system, etc. that everyone respects or accepts without questioning

examples.x: Many traditional customs and practices are still treated as sacred cows in this society.
The politician refuses to address the issue, treating it as a sacred cow.
The organization finally challenged its sacred cows and implemented much-needed reforms.
Over time, societal attitudes have shifted, and some previously held sacred cows have been questioned.
If we want progress, we need to be willing to question our sacred cows in the coming years.
In the future, the political landscape may see a shift as previously untouchable sacred cows are challenged.

senses.definition: a  limit that makes clear what is considered right or accepted and what is not

examples.x: They draw a line in the sand when it comes to protecting the environment.
The CEO sets a clear line in the sand on ethical business practices.
She drew a line in the sand and ended the toxic relationship.
The government officials set a firm line in the sand during the negotiations.
He established a clear line in the sand, and everyone respected his decision.
She is determined to establish a line in the sand for her personal well-being.
The organization plans to set a strong line in the sand regarding employee safety.

senses.definition: to behave in a manner that is in contrast to the standards of a particular place

examples.x: He breaks every rule in the book and faces the consequences of his rebellious actions.
They will break every rule in the book to achieve their goals, undeterred by conventions.
The rebellious teenager decided to break every rule in the book and stay out past curfew, skip school, and engage in risky behaviors.

senses.definition: the socially accepted or expected behavior or action in a particular situation

examples.x: Wearing formal attire to the event is the done thing.
In their culture, offering a small gift when visiting someone's home is the done thing.
It was not the done thing to arrive late for the meeting.
Following the established protocol is the done thing in diplomatic circles.
Expressing gratitude for a kind gesture is the done thing in many societies.

senses.definition: a person's natural tendency or urge to conform to the standards or the behavior of others in a particular group or society

examples.x: Many people exhibit a herd mentality on social media, following popular trends without considering their individual values.
The employees' decisions were influenced by herd mentality, as they all adopted the same approach without questioning its effectiveness.
During the financial crisis, herd mentality led investors to panic and sell off their stocks in a mass sell-off.
If the group leader encourages critical thinking and individuality, they can help prevent herd mentality from dominating decision-making.
If people succumb to herd mentality without questioning the consequences, they may overlook alternative perspectives and miss valuable insights.

word_list.pronunciation: swˈɪm ɡˌoʊ ɐɡˈɛnst ðə tˈaɪd stɹˈiːm

senses.definition: to do or think the exact opposite of what most people are doing or thinking

examples.x: Despite widespread criticism, she chose to swim against the tide and pursue her unconventional artistic style.
In a world driven by materialism, he decided to swim against the tide and prioritize experiences over possessions.
The political activist was known for his courage to swim against the tide and challenge the established norms.
The young entrepreneur's decision to go against the tide and start a business in a declining industry proved to be a wise move.
The scientist's groundbreaking research went against the tide of prevailing scientific theories at the time.
It can be challenging to swim against the stream, but sometimes it's necessary to maintain personal integrity.
Despite societal pressures, she decided to swim against the stream and pursue a career in a male-dominated field.
The author's controversial book went against the stream of popular opinion, sparking intense debate and discussion.
The innovative start-up decided to go against the stream and disrupt the established norms of the industry.
They chose to go against the stream and challenge the status quo, resulting in significant changes within the organization.
It's not easy to go against the stream, but sometimes it's necessary to bring about meaningful change.
The athlete's unconventional training methods went against the stream but ultimately led to outstanding performance.

senses.definition: to do things according to the rules, customs, or in the way that is expected of one in order to achieve profit or success

examples.x: She plays the game by networking and building connections to advance her career.
They play the game by adhering to the company's policies and procedures to maintain a professional reputation.
He plays the game by studying the market trends and adjusting his strategies accordingly to stay competitive.
Despite facing challenges, she played the game by persevering and eventually achieved her business goals.
They played the game by following the industry standards and gained recognition for their innovative approach.
He played the game by collaborating with key stakeholders and secured the necessary support for his project.

senses.definition: to start behaving in a manner that ignores the expectations of society or an authority

examples.x: She often goes rogue in team meetings, challenging conventional ideas and proposing alternative solutions.
They go rogue by implementing innovative marketing strategies that defy industry norms.
He frequently goes rogue in his fashion choices, opting for bold and unconventional outfits.
Despite warnings, she went rogue and launched her own startup, defying the expectations of her corporate career.
He went rogue in the political campaign, deviating from the party's agenda to address grassroots concerns.
He will go rogue in his next film project, exploring experimental storytelling techniques.
If she had the opportunity, she would go rogue and revolutionize the industry with her disruptive ideas.

senses.definition: to act in a way that goes beyond the limits of what is allowed or accepted

examples.x: He consistently oversteps the mark with his sarcastic and hurtful remarks.
They overstepped the line in the previous project, causing delays and budget overruns.
He realized he had overstepped the line with his prying questions and apologized.
She will likely overstep the mark if she continues to disregard the boundaries set by her superiors.
He is at risk of overstepping the line if he doesn't exercise restraint in his criticism.
